title: Contact Center Overview
lede: The Contact Center is where your human agents take and make phone calls. This guide walks you through the four tabs, the Live Monitor, and how supervisors can listen in on live calls.

steps.title: Open the Contact Center
steps.content:
1. In the left navigation, open Call Center.
  2. Click Contact Center.
  3. You land on a workspace with up to four tabs across the top: Live Monitor, Reporting, Groups, and Call Transfer.

Which tabs you see depends on your Contact Center role. Agents see only Live Monitor (as a queue view), Monitor/QA also see Reporting, and Admins see all four including Groups and Call Transfer.

steps.title: Watch live calls in Live Monitor
steps.content:
The Live Monitor tab is your real-time wallboard:

  • Stat tiles at the top show Ringing / queued, On calls, Agents online, On break, Busy, and On queue counts.
  • Live Calls lists every active call with its parties, direction, and a running clock. On Queue lists callers waiting for an agent.
  • The Human Agents roster on the right shows every agent with a colored status dot: green = online, amber = break, blue = busy, grey = offline.

images.caption: Live Monitor: wallboard tiles, live calls, and the Human Agents roster.
Live Monitor: wallboard tiles, live calls, and the Human Agents roster.

steps.title: Monitor, Whisper, or Barge a call
steps.content:
Supervisors and admins can join any live call from its card:

  • Monitor — silently listen; nobody on the call hears you.
  • Whisper — talk privately to your agent (the customer never hears you).
  • Barge — join the conversation as a full three-way participant.

You can also Mute AI agent or Terminate the call. Monitor/QA users get listen-only Monitor; agents get no supervisor controls.

steps.title: Understand agent statuses
steps.content:
Each agent sets their own availability from the web dialer:

  • Online — ready to receive queued and inbound calls.
  • On break — signed in but not receiving calls.
  • Offline — signed out of the queue.

Supervisors can force an agent Ready, Not Ready (break), or log them out from the roster's action buttons.

steps.title: Review performance in Reporting
steps.content: The Reporting tab summarizes call activity across six categories — General, Human Agents, AI Agents, Smart Campaigns, Human Campaigns, and Queue — with KPIs like total calls, connect rate, average handle time, and cost. Pick a date preset and export any tab to CSV.

images.caption: Reporting: KPI cards, category tabs, date presets, and CSV export.
Reporting: KPI cards, category tabs, date presets, and CSV export.

support.title: Need Help?
support.content:
If you run into any issues or have questions:

  • 📧 Email us at support@imash.io
  • 💬 Join our Discord community
  • 📚 Check out our API Reference
  • 🎥 Watch our video tutorials
